rulings.Case Title: ELBA LUCERO et al VS. DALE T. BOUTIETTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Defts Supplemental Discovery Requests And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Tuesday, July 16, 2013, Line 1: Defendants DALE BOUTIETTE'S, BOUTIETTE + VON HERMANN, LLP'S, and BVH LAW GROUP, LLP'S Motion to Compel Responses to Defendants Supplemental Discovery Requests and for Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted; sanctions imposed in the amount of $450. Responses to be served within 30 days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ELBA LUCERO et al VS. DALE T. BOUTIETTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Toc Ompel Further Responses To Defts' Request For Documents, Set Three And For Reques For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Tuesday, July 16, 2013, Line 1: Defendants DALE BOUTIETTE'S, BOUTIETTE + VON HERMANN, LLP'S, and BVH LAW GROUP, LLP'S Motion to Compel Further Responses to Defendants' Request for Documents, Set Three and for Request for Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties are directed to have one final meet and confer discussion via telephone by 4pm today to attempt to resolve the dispute. The Court notes that as originally drafted the RFPDs were overbroad, however, both parties appear to suggest an appropriate compromise where the two electronic files at issue would be produced in ¿native format,¿ and no forensic computer examination would be required. If the matter is resolved during the meet and confer today, and the parties agree that the motion may be taken off calendar, the moving party shall notify the Pro Tem Judge (davidmcdonald@prodigy.net) and Department 302 (contestdept302tr@sftc.org) via email by 430pm, and no hearing will be required. =(302)

rulings.Case Title: PRO UNLIMITED INC. VS. INTEGRITEAM INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, July 16, 2013, Line 9, CROSS DEFENDANT PRO UNLIMITED INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-Defendant Pro Unlimited Inc.'s Demurrer to Cross-Complainant Campbell & Spangler's First Amended Cross-Complaint ("FACC") is overruled. Cross-Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Cross-Defendant's demurrer to the first cause of action is overruled. Cross-Complainant alleges that Integriteam, Inc. was acting as an agent for Cross-Defendant when Integriteam, Inc. entered into a consulting agreement with Cross-Complainant for consulting services for which Cross-Defendant Pro was the intended beneficiary. (FACC 5.) Cross-Defendant allegedly breached the consulting agreement when it failed to pay Cross-Complainant for consulting services rendered. (FACC 15.) Cross-Defendant's demurrer to the second cause of action is overruled. Cross-Complainant has pled sufficient facts to state a cause of action for quantum meruit. Cross-Complainant is permitted to plead inconsistent legal theories. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A. VS. WENY Z BERNARDINO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Discovery Responses And Req. For Sanctions; Ntc Of Non Appearance
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Tuesday, July 16, 2013, Line 3: Plaintiff CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A.'S Motion to Compel Discovery Responses and Request for Sanctions; Notice of Non Appearance Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed; motion granted; sanctions of $200 imposed against Defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
